channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court blocks new deportations of Venezuelans in Texas under 18th century Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court blocked, at least for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Partisan divide widening on US support for NATO: Pew
item.description: Among party lines, Democrats' opinion about NATO being beneficial to the U.S. has largely stayed the same, but this view has dropped off by 6 points among GOP respondents.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio says State Department to restrict visas of at least 250 Nicaraguan officials
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced the State Department will restrict visas for at least 250 Nicaraguan officials, pointing to human rights violations made by the Nicaraguan government, led by co-presidents Daniel Ortega and Rosario Murillo, who are husband and wife.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Bill would require lawmakers to put some investments in blind trust
item.description: “Members of Congress should get out of the business of buying and selling stocks,” Rep. Greg Landsman, D-Ohio, said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans deny Democrats' requests for El Salvador oversight trips
item.description: House Republican committee chairs are denying Democrats’ requests to travel to El Salvador and visit its CECOT prison facility as scrutiny builds on the Trump administration’s handling of Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s mistaken deportation.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves to invoke Schedule F to make it easier to fire some federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has begun making one of the controversial personnel changes for government employees that was spelled out in the conservative Project 2025 blueprint for his second term.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge pauses Trump administration's plans for mass layoffs at Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: The decision leaves in limbo a bureau created after the Great Recession to safeguard against fraud, abuse and deceptive practices. Trump administration officials argue that it has overstepped its authority and should have a more limited mission.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration from passport changes affecting some transgender Americans
item.description: A federal judge has partially blocked the Trump administration from enacting a policy that bans the use of “X” marker used by many nonbinary people on passports as well as the changing of gender markers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge says detained Tufts student must be transferred from Louisiana to Vermont
item.description: A federal judge has ordered that a Tufts University student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities in March be brought to Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Holly Ramer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge declines to further enforce order restoring AP access to White House
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to further enforce his order directing the White House to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces, signaling that while top officials are owed a presumption of good faith, violations of his directive would be met with a heavy hand. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House photo bluntly shows where parties stand on immigration amid Abrego Garcia deportation
item.description: The White House releases contrasting images showing Trump comforting grieving mother Patty Morin while Democrat senator Van Hollen meets with deported illegal immigrant in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harvard records requested by Education Department
item.description: The records request comes after the Trump administration has made multiple moves against the Ivy League.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin governor can lock in 400-year school funding increase using a veto, court says
item.description: The Wisconsin Supreme Court has ruled that the Democratic governor legally used the state’s uniquely powerful veto to lock in a school funding increase for 400 years. The split ruling from the liberal-controlled court on Friday affirms the partial veto power of Wisconsin governors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says Ukraine-Russia talks 'coming to a head' and 'no one is playing' him in push to end war
item.description: President Donald Trump says negotiations between Ukraine and Russia are “coming to a head” and insists that neither side is “playing” him in his push to end the grinding war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Career diplomat becomes the face of Trump's 'America First' agenda at the UN
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The highest-ranking U.S. representative now at the United Nations told Congress two years ago that Russia's invasion of Ukraine was “unprovoked" and “unjustified,” urging U.N. members to condemn Moscow’s aggression and demand an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In their words: What judges and Trump's government say about Abrego Garcia's mistaken deportation
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has dug in on its contention that the government should not have to repatriate Kilmar Abrego Garcia, despite U.S. Supreme Court and lower court rulings that he was wrongly deported and should be returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hope Florida Foundation meeting disrupted by slurs, pornographic images amid scrutiny
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Pornographic images and racial slurs abruptly ended the virtual Hope Florida Foundation meeting Thursday morning. Board members then reconvened later in the afternoon for an hour and 30 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Noem threatens Harvard with ban on foreign student visas
item.description: Noem sent a “scathing letter” to Harvard demanding the university hand over records of foreign students.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Clashing Over Campus Control: Lawmakers vs. DeSantis on transparency
item.description: For years, lawmakers have speculated that the leaders who run the state's Universities and Colleges have been gifted these positions based on their political loyalties to the Governor.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protest of Lombardo's 'freeloaders' comment planned Saturday in downtown Las Vegas
item.description: A rally planned Saturday afternoon in downtown Las Vegas is stepping up the pushback against Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's remarks that freeloaders are behind opposition to policy changes.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Keri Heintzeman wins Republican primary in race to fill Justin Eichorn’s state Senate seat
item.description: The winner of the April 29 special election will fill the District 6 seat left vacant by Justin Eichorn, who resigned last month after he was charged with soliciting a minor.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Pritzker says he's undecided about testifying before Congressional panel
item.description: The US House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform requested testimony from Pritzker, other Democrats about sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9
